abstract.text: Returns a new file path made by removing the extension from this file
path.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the current process appears
able to delete the file-system node at this file path.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the current process appears
able to read the file-system node at this file path.
Reads the contents of the regular file at this file path.
Returns a new file path made by replacing the current extension of this
file path with the provided extension.
Matches the provided glob pattern against the file system.
Appends an extension separator, followed by the provided extension, to
this file path.
Copies the file-system node at this file path to the new location
synchronously.
Replaces the contents of the file-system node at this file path in a
manner that ensures no data loss occurs.
Removes extraneous components from this file path.
Returns the date at which the file-system node at this file path was
most recently accessed.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the current process appears
able to execute the file-system node at this file path.
Recursively determines the total size in bytes of the file-system node
at this file path, including any descendants.
Creates a hard link between the file-system node at this file path and
the file-system node at the provided file path.
Returns the file path of the file-system node pointed to by the symbolic
link at this file path.
Returns a new file path made by resolving all symbolic links in this
file path and standardizing it.
Returns the attributes of the file-system node at this file path.
Replaces the current extension of this file path with the provided
extension.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether a file-system node exists at
this file path.
Returns the kind of the file-system node at this file path.
Returns a new file path made by replacing the current home directory
portion of this file path with a tilde (~) character.
Resolves all symbolic links in this file path and standardizes it.
Writes the provided data to the regular file at this file path.
Performs a shallow search of the directory at this file path and returns
file paths for the contained items.
Creates a directory with the provided attributes at this file path.
The file path to the current working directory.
Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the file-system nodes in
this file path and the provided file path have the same contents.
Creates a symbolic link at this file path that points to the file-system
node at the provided file path.
Creates a new temporary replacement directory appropriate for this file
path.
Unzips the archive file at this file path into the directory at the
provided file path.
Removes the file-system node at this file path.
The file URL equivalent to the file path.
A representation of a file’s kind.
A container of file attribute values.
Returns the date at which the file-system node at this file path was
created.
Returns a new file path made by making this file path absolute.
Returns a new file path made by expanding the initial component of this
file path to its full path value.
Expands the initial component of this file path to its full path value.
Zips the file-system node contents at this file path into the archive
file at the provided file path.
Returns a new file path made by removing extraneous components from this
file path.
Miscellaneous tools to ease writing code.
Matches the provided glob pattern relative to this file path.
The base name of this file path.
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the current process appears
able to write to the file-system node at this file path.
Removes the extension from this file path.
The file path of a globally unique temporary directory for the process.
Returns the date at which the file-system node at this file path was
most recently modified.
Sets some or all od the attributes of the file-system node at this file
path.
Moves the file-system node at this file path to the new location
synchronously.
Replaces the current home directory portion of this file path with a
tilde (~) character.
Returns a new file path made by appending an extension separator,
followed by the provided extension, to this file path.
The file path of the temporary directory for the current user.
